items.title: King praises frontliners for dedication during Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, paid a heartfelt tribute to the nation's security forces and enforcement agencies, praising their unwavering commitment and selfless sacrifices during the upcoming Aidilfitri festive season. Sultan Ibrahim highlighted the dedication shown by these personnel, noting that they carry out their duties with a strong sense of responsibility to ensure public peace, order, and the well-being of the rakyat remain uncompromised. Earlier today, Sultan Ibrahim granted an audience to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at Istana Bukit Tunku to discuss national matters.

items.title: Family escapes unharmed after car catches fire en route for Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA KRAI: A family of five travelling from Nilai to their hometown in Kok Lanas for Aidilfitri had a narrow escape when their car caught fire in Kampung Pahi, near Hospital Sultan Ismail Petra, last night. Kuala Krai Fire and Rescue Station chief Senior Fire Officer Nik Ahmad Afsham Nik Pa said the station received an emergency call at 10.48pm. Nik Ahmad Afsham added that the Kuala Krai Assistance Squad helped transport the family safely to Kok Lanas.

items.title: IGP: Stay calm, avoid inflaming tensions over unauthorised houses of worship
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail today urged Malaysians to remain calm over the issue of unauthorised houses of worship, warning that rising rhetoric risks inflaming racial tensions if left unchecked. He said what is fundamentally an administrative matter involving land ownership, local authority approvals and compliance with planning laws has increasingly spilled into the public sphere, where it is framed along religious and racial lines. Efforts like this help ensure racial harmony and national stability,” he said.

items.title: Four fined RM750 each over drunken brawl
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Four men were fined RM750 each by the Magistrate's Court for their involvement in a drunken brawl at an entertainment outlet last week. Nai Siang, 39, See Chee Rong, 29, Ong Poh Heng, 31, and Ter Aik Siong, 32, pleaded guilty before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass. All four paid the fine.

items.title: IGP: Activist Tamim may have fled to Saudi Arabia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — The police claimed that activist Tamim Dahri Abdul Razak may have fled to Saudi Arabia and are actively tracking his whereabouts, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. He said checks indicated Tamim had travelled to Thailand and is now believed to be in Saudi Arabia. Earlier this week, Mohd Khalid confirmed that four individuals — including Zamri Vinoth and activist Arun Dorasamy — would be charged in connection with alleged religious provocation.

items.title: Malaysia's inflation modestly up 1.4pct in Feb 2026: Statistics Department
items.summary: These included insurance and financial services at 4.7 per cent (January 2026: 5.5 per cent), education 2.8 per cent (3.2 per cent), restaurant and accommodation services 2.5 per cent (3.0 per cent), and food and beverages 1.3 per cent (1.5 per cent). Other groups showed marginal increases, with health rising 1.2 per cent (1.4 per cent), housing, water, electricity, gas, and other fuels up 1.1 per cent (1.2 per cent), and information and communication advancing 0.5 per cent (0.7 per cent). Meanwhile, personal care, social protection, and miscellaneous goods and services increased to 6.9 per cent (6.6 per cent), while alcoholic beverages and tobacco rose to 2.6 per cent (2.5 per cent).

items.title: Lessons in asymmetrical warfare concept in modern conflicts
items.summary: LETTER: THOMAS Edward Lawrence, famously known as Lawrence of Arabia, left an enduring legacy through his mastery of guerrilla tactics during the First World War. Next, innovation in warfare must transcend the narrow confines of traditional military personnel or industries, specifically those specialising in artificial intelligence (AI), cyber-security, and drone technology. Asymmetrical warfare often evolves into gruelling wars of attrition.

items.title: Non-national carmakers face pressure amid rising competition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: National carmakers are poised to sustain sales momentum throughout the year, while non-national players may continue to face pressure, according to Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB). "Order backlogs remain strong amid robust demand for national marques. The firm named MBM Resources Bhd and Sime Darby Bhd as its top picks in the automotive sector.

items.title: IGP: Police to record statements from 14 more individuals in alleged plot to topple govt probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Police may soon call in up to 14 more individuals to assist in investigations into an alleged attempt to topple the government and acts of sabotage against national stability, Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. He said police expect to record statements from all identified individuals as part of ongoing investigations. “Investigations are still ongoing and the police are examining the matter while carrying out several follow-up actions.

items.title: Bahulu gets modern makeover to win over younger generation
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A traditional kuih is getting a contemporary update, as one entrepreneur reinvents bahulu with modern flavours to appeal to a younger generation. Bahulu Sendeng founder Siti Suhana Mohd Daralsalam, 42, has introduced 13 variations of the classic treat, including Nutella, chocolate, pandan, strawberry and red velvet. Siti Suhana said the idea came from a desire to expand her customer base.

items.title: IGP: Police treat Zamri Vinoth, Arun Dorasamy equally
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have denied treating Zamri Vinoth and Arun Dorasamy differently, emphasising that action was taken strictly in accordance with the law. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said that although all four individuals were arrested under Section 505(b) of the Penal Code, one of the cases also involved an offence under the Sedition Act 1948. These actions are based on the legal provisions," he told reporters at Bukit Aman today.

items.title: New Sungai Krian Bridge opens, ending long wait for Pusa and Kabong residents, says Uggah
items.summary: KUCHING, March 19 — The long wait for residents of Pusa and Kabong to cross Sungai Krian has ended with the official opening of the Sungai Krian Bridge yesterday. Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas (5th right) receives a memento from Cassidy after the Sungai Krian Bridge soft launch ceremony March 19, 2026. — Picture via Facebook/JKR Sarawak Meanwhile, the Public Works Department (JKR) Sarawak said the Sungai Krian Bridge project, part of the Sarawak Coastal Road under Package D4, was executed with a contract value of RM112.5 million.

items.title: Two charged with neighbour's murder
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Two men were charged in the Magistrate's Court here today with the murder of their neighbour on March 10. No plea was recorded as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court. Rayer requested the court to allow his clients to receive medical treatment for foot injuries allegedly sustained while in custody.

items.title: AstraZeneca introduces rare disease drug Soliris in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AstraZeneca has made its rare disease treatment, Soliris, available in Malaysia, expanding options for patients with complex neurological autoimmune conditions and severe blood disorders. AstraZeneca Malaysia country president Svetlana Yanchuk said the treatment's availability reflects the company's commitment to supporting patients living with rare diseases. "With Soliris, we can advance our efforts in Malaysia to transform the lives of people living with rare diseases and help them live more meaningful lives," she said.

items.title: Malaysia trade surplus jumps 137.6pct to RM38.7bil in Jan-Feb
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's trade surplus widened 137.6 per cent to RM38.67 billion in the first two months of this year, as total trade rose 11 per cent to RM516.89 billion, Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) said. Export growth was driven by the electrical and electronics sector, which expanded 34.2 per cent and accounted for nearly half of total exports at RM131.34 billion. Machinery, equipment and parts rose 39.8 per cent to RM12.49 billion, while palm oil and agricultural products increased 6.6 per cent to RM12.41 billion.

items.title: Chegubard expelled from Bersatu, says party no longer serves rakyat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu Port Dickson chief Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, has been expelled from the party. He said the party's disciplinary board secretary informed him via WhatsApp at 6pm yesterday. Chegubard claimed Bersatu no longer champions the people's cause and instead serves certain groups.

items.title: IGP warns against racial provocation, urges restraint
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have urged the public to refrain from making provocative statements on race and religion, warning that such remarks could threaten national stability. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said recent developments showed how administrative issues could quickly escalate into racial and religious tensions if not handled carefully. I urge all parties to reduce negative comments that touch on religion and race," he told reporters at Bukit Aman on Thursday.

items.title: Victor Chin abroad amid money laundering probe, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are continuing their investigation into businessman Victor Chin under anti-money laundering laws, with the suspect believed to be abroad. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the probe was being conducted under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act (Amla). Previously, Bukit Aman Anti-Money Laundering Crime Investigation Unit chief Datuk Fazlisyam Abdul Majid said the raids also resulted in the seizure of several documents relevant to the probe.

items.title: Over 3 million vehicles expected on KLK, LPT1 during Aidilfitri
items.summary: Pahang Public Works, Transport, and Health Committee chairman Datuk Razali Kassim said the surge in traffic is projected to occur over a 12-day period between March 18 and March 29. "The average daily peak traffic was estimated at 78,000 vehicles. However, we anticipated the highest traffic volume to occur on March 28, with approximately 106,000 vehicles on the road," he said.

items.title: Culvert installation underway to tackle flooding at Jalan Hua Lai, Jalan Hock Ming in Bawang Assan, says SUPP
items.summary: SIBU, March 19 — Sarawak United Peoples’ Party (SUPP) Bawang Assan branch has initiated an infrastructure improvement project along Jalan Hua Lai and Jalan Hock Ming, involving the installation of culverts. The branch’s chairman Cr Kevin Lau said the project aims to enhance the local drainage system and address long-standing flooding issues faced by residents in the area. The affected roads have long suffered from poor drainage, resulting in frequent flooding during heavy rainfall.

items.title: Businessman Victor Chin accuses police of ‘unguided bombardment’ in AMLA probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Businessman Victor Chin Boon Long today renewed his criticism of the Royal Malaysia Police’s (PDRM) Anti-Money Laundering (AMLA) unit, accusing it of “persecuting innocent people” and heavy-handedness in its investigation against him. “Anyone who knows me becomes collateral damage,” Chin claimed. “We are living in an environment where anyone who knows Victor Chin or calls Victor Chin their boss, gets AMLA-ed.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police continue search for activist Tamim believed to be abroad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracking activist Tamim Dahri Abdul Razak, believed to have fled the country, with intelligence indicating he may have travelled to Thailand or the Middle East. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said authorities were still working to locate the individual. A total of 192 police reports had been lodged against Tamim.

items.title: Lorry driver charged over Bangsar armed gang robbery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A lorry driver pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to a charge of armed gang robbery at an Ayurvedic clinic in Bangsar. Sangara Lingam Packiry, 50, entered the plea after the charge was read out before judge Suhaila Haron. "However, should the court exercise its discretion to grant bail, the prosecution requests a sum of RM10,000 with additional conditions, namely reporting to the nearest police station and not harassing prosecution witnesses," she said.

items.title: ETS tickets 86pc already sold as KTMB boosts trips for Raya travel
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 19 — A total of 217,000 Electric Train Service (ETS) tickets for the Hari Raya Aidilfitri travel period have been sold as of 9 am today, out of 257,000 tickets offered. Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) Chief Operating Officer Afzar Zakariya said the figure represents about 86 per cent of total ticket sales, with around 35,000 tickets still available, mainly for trips into Kuala Lumpur. “It can be said that tickets for balik kampung journeys are almost gone, while tickets for trips into Kuala Lumpur are still available,” he said after inspecting operations at JB Sentral today.

items.title: [UPDATED] Indira Gandhi case: IGP vows continued probe after committal bid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have pledged to continue investigating the long-standing Indira Gandhi case following a committal application filed against the office of the Inspector-General of Police. He said that police remain committed to assisting in resolving the case, while acknowledging that legal action taken by Indira is her right. The move stems from a 2014 Ipoh High Court recovery order which compelled police to find Indira's daughter, Prasana Diksa, and arrest her ex-husband, Muhammad Riduan Abdullah.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police seek international cooperation in Bloomberg probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are seeking international cooperation in a probe involving Bloomberg, with some individuals linked to the case believed to be based overseas. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said officers had visited Bloomberg's office at Menara Petronas and interviewed several staff members. Meanwhile, news portal MalaysiaNow reported that police had visited Bloomberg's Kuala Lumpur office to identify the journalist behind investigative reports on Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki's shareholdings.

items.title: Natural Resources Ministry pledges support for family of border survey officer swept away in Sarawak river, says Arthur Kurup
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 19 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) has assured that the welfare of the family of a Department of Survey and Mapping Malaysia (JUPEM) officer who went missing in Sungai Pandaruan, Limbang, will remain a priority throughout the ongoing search and rescue operation. Minister, Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup, said he has instructed the department to extend all necessary assistance, including moral support and welfare coordination, to the victim’s family. Arthur said he had been informed of the tragic incident involving a JUPEM officer who was reportedly swept away by strong currents while carrying out boundary surveying work along the Malaysia–Brunei border at Sungai Pandaruan.

items.title: Police widen probe into alleged plot to topple government
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have expanded their investigation into an alleged plot to topple the government and undermine national stability, recording statements from 14 individuals so far. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said investigators are continuing efforts to identify and call up more people believed to be linked to the case. "We have taken statements from 14 individuals so far, and there are about 14 more we may call," he told reporters at Bukit Aman on Thursday.

items.title: Fire razes 10 homes in Lutong, Sarawak; 42 homeless as firefighters battle dense layout (VIDEO)
items.summary: MIRI, March 19 — A devastating fire at the Kampung Pasir squatter colony in Lutong here early this morning has left 42 villagers homeless. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) said it received an emergency call at 4.06am and dispatched firefighters from the Lutong fire station, assisted by teams from Lopeng and Miri Central. “The fire eventually razed 10 houses to the ground, nine of which were occupied, displacing families comprising 42 individuals.

items.title: Dodol gula kabung keeps tradition alive in Raub
items.summary: Mohd Isa Ishak, 74, a retired government officer, remained active by producing dodol gula kabung (palm sugar) entirely by hand. According to him, making dodol manually required considerable physical effort and served as excellent cardio exercise. Every Aidilfitri, Mohd Isa limited orders for his dodol gula kabung to maintain the quality of production.

items.title: Top Glove faces margin pressure amid rising costs, stronger ringgit: HLIB Research
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Top Glove Corp Bhd is expected to face a challenging third quarter of the financial year ending Aug 31, 2026 (Q3 FY26) amid margin pressure from rising raw material costs and a stronger ringgit, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB Research) said. HLIB Research trimmed its FY26–28 forecasts by 6.9 per cent, 2.0 per cent and 1.1 per cent, respectively, citing potential flattish sales volumes and rising input costs. HLIB Research, however, cautions that these price adjustments may not fully offset the ringgit's 7.5 per cent appreciation against the US dollar since late October 2025 and ongoing cost pressures.

items.title: Yen under pressure as BOJ and Fed hold rates steady
items.summary: SINGAPORE: The yen teetered on the brink of its weakest level in two years in Asian morning trading on Thursday, struggling to find footing against a resurgent dollar as the Bank of Japan held rates steady in the shadow of the Iran conflict. The yen was 0.1 per cent firmer at 159.655 per dollar, off its weakest levels in two years, as the BOJ kept its rate at 0.75 per cent and maintained its assessment that the economy was recovering moderately. Attention turns next to a press conference in a few hours where BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda will explain the central bank's decision.

items.title: Hari Raya Aidilfitri @ Istana Arau celebration falls on first day of Syawal
items.summary: KANGAR: The Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebration at Istana Arau will be held from 9.30am to noon on the first day of Syawal, following the Aidilfitri prayer. It said Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin, the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil, will also perform the Aidilfitri prayer, scheduled to begin at 8 am, at Dataran Istiadat Istana Arau. Attendees are advised to bring their own prayer mats, arrive in a state of ablution or perform ablution beforehand at the Arau State Mosque, and carry shoe bags for convenience.

items.title: Retrenched factory worker finds success making 'capal' sandals
items.summary: KANGAR: What began as a setback after losing his factory job during the economic downturn in 2000 turned into a blessing in disguise for Saad Saep, who now earns a living crafting traditional Malay capal sandals. Saad, 60, from Kampung Chemumar Tengah in Simpang Empat near here, said he never expected the loss of his job at a plastic component manufacturing factory to lead him to a new path as a capal maker. Recalling how it all began, Saad said he first learned the craft from a friend while living in Sungai Petani, Kedah, before eventually gaining the confidence to produce and sell the traditional footwear independently at night markets and bazaars.

items.title: Malaysia's macro position is stronger to weather shock compared to pandemic era
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's stronger ringgit is helping to ease the impact of rising global import prices, although the country's import cover has declined, compared with pre-COVID 19 pandemic levels, said the World Bank's lead economist for Malaysia, Dr Apurva Sanghi. He said Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) growth has risen to 5.2 per cent in 2025 from 4.4 per cent in 2019, while real GDP per capita increased to RM48,200 from RM42,500 previously. Inflation stood at 1.6 per cent currently versus 0.7 per cent in 2019, while the fiscal deficit has widened slightly to 3.8 per cent of GDP from 3.4 per cent previously, he said, while government debt has also increased to 64.7 per cent of GDP from 52.4 per cent.

items.title: Diesel subsidy review: Expert urges targeted, data-driven approach
items.summary: Professor Dr Asan Ali Golam Hassan of Universiti Teknologi Malaysia's Azman Hashim International Business School said direct subsidies aimed at specific sectors or groups would be more effective than broad price controls. "A managed pricing system, backed by appropriate social protection measures, could also help reduce market distortions without causing major disruptions," he told New Straits Times. Asan Ali noted that Sabah and Sarawak are particularly vulnerable to diesel smuggling due to their geographic and economic conditions.

items.title: Perlis’ Hari Raya Aidilfitri @ Istana Arau celebration set for first day of Syawal
items.summary: KANGAR, March 19 — The Hari Raya Aidilfitri @ Istana Arau celebration at Istana Arau will be held at Dataran Majlis, Istana Arau, from 9.30am to noon on the first day of Syawal after the Aidilfitri prayer. It said Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin, the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil, will also perform the Aidilfitri prayer, scheduled to begin at 8 am, at Dataran Istiadat Istana Arau. Attendees are advised to bring their own prayer mats, arrive in a state of ablution or perform ablution beforehand at the Arau State Mosque, and bring shoe bags for convenience.

items.title: Penang childcare worker charged with murder of four‑month‑old boy at HSJ centre
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 19 — A childcare centre worker was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a four-month-old baby boy. Nur Farhana Fazmar Abdullah, 22, nodded after the charge was read out before Magistrate Nurul Rasyidah Mohd Akit, but no plea was recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutor Siti Nur Amalina Harun, while lawyer Muhaimin Hashim represented the accused.

items.title: LLM: Traffic heading to east coast, north congested but under control
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic flow out of the Klang Valley heading to the north and east coast was reported to be congested but remained under control this morning. A spokesman for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said on the east coast route, congestion occurred on the Middle Ring Road 2 towards the Gombak Toll Plaza, as well as from Bukit Tinggi and Lentang to the Bentong Petronas station, and from Sungai Dua to the Karak Toll Plaza. "For the route to the north, congestion occurred on the North-South Expressway from Slim River to Tapah, Gopeng to Simpang Pulai, Ipoh North to the Menora Tunnel, Bandar Baharu to Jawi, Bukit Tambun to Bukit Minyak and Juru to Perai," he said when contacted here today.

items.title: Happiness rising: Malaysia outpaces Singapore and global average in latest Ipsos study
items.summary: Malaysia's improved score places it comfortably above the global average of 74 per cent, reflecting a broadly positive national mood despite international uncertainties. Most Southeast Asian nations recorded similar gains, with Indonesia topping the global charts at 85 per cent, the highest happiness score of any country measured. High-income Malaysians reported the strongest happiness outcomes, with 87 per cent describing themselves as happy — ten points above the global high-income average of 79 per cent.

items.title: Melaka crime index up 2.9pc to 318 cases, violent crimes rise 6.3pc
items.summary: MELAKA, March 19 — Crime index in Melaka rose 2.9 per cent to 318 cases from January 1 to March 16 compared to 309 cases in the same period last year. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said of the total, violent crimes including rape and causing injury increased by 6.3 per cent to 67 cases in the same period this year compared to 63 cases last year. “Likewise, property crimes have also increased by two per cent to 251 cases in the same period this year compared to 246 cases last year, which involves an increase in theft and motorcycle theft cases,” he told reporters here this morning.

items.title: Price tags are for all, says KPDN in Melaka cooking oil raid
items.summary: MELAKA: A routine enforcement operation by the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) escalated into a tense exchange when an officer reminded a cashier that price tags must be displayed for all customers, not just Malaysians, during an inspection involving subsidised cooking oil. The operation, captured in a video shared by KPDN Melaka on Facebook, showed officers checking the sale and display of subsidised cooking oil at a retail premises. During the inspection, the officer confronted workers over the absence and inconsistency of clearly displayed price tags on goods offered to customers.

items.title: HSBC flags 20,000 job cuts as AI overhaul unfolds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: HSBC Holdings Plc is weighing a wave of deep job cuts over the coming years that could ultimately impact around 20,000 roles, or about 10 per cent of its total workforce, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter. HSBC didn't immediately respond to a Reuters' request for a comment. HSBC employed 208,720 full-time equivalent staff at the end of December 2025, according to its annual report.

items.title: UN appoints Malaysia’s Dr Adeeba to high-level panel to review global drug policies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Malaysian public health expert Professor Emeritus Datuk Dr Adeeba Kamarulzaman has been appointed by United Nations Secretary-General Antonio Guterres to an international panel tasked with evaluating global drug policies. The panel will provide key recommendations for a landmark global review of drug policy commitments scheduled for 2029. Describing the appointment as a “once-in-a-lifetime opportunity,” Dr Adeeba said the panel’s work is crucial to determine if international drug policies genuinely protect public health and human well-being.

items.title: In Raya missive, CM says respect for Ramadan during CNY a sign of Penang’s unique harmony
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 19 — Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow has hailed Penang’s celebrated multicultural harmony as its greatest strength, pointing to the modest celebration of Chinese New Year during Ramadan this year as a palpable example of the state’s spirit of mutual respect. In his Hari Raya Aidilfitri address today, Chow said the festive season was a prime opportunity to cultivate the “values of love and unity” which form the foundation of life in Penang, an island known for its “atmosphere of mutual respect for the diversity of religions, cultures and backgrounds.” “The year 2026 is especially remarkable, as the Chinese New Year celebration was held simultaneously with Ramadan al-Mubarak,” Chow said. “What I observed was that Chinese New Year events were celebrated with modesty out of respect for this blessed month.” The chief minister reflected on his own experiences attending iftar gatherings across the state, where he met a wide cross-section of society, from educators and farmers to religious teachers and children with special needs.

items.title: Ipoh police tracking man seen in viral video attempting cable theft
items.summary: IPOH, March 19 — Police are actively tracking a man believed to be involved in the attempted theft of cables on Jalan Medan Istana here after a video recording of the incident went viral on social media on Tuesday. Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said his team detected a video uploaded by the account owner “update11111” on platform X, showing a suspect believed to be committing the act. Therefore, he said police are actively tracking the suspect, and anyone with information regarding the incident can contact the nearest police station or the Ipoh district police station headquarters (IPD).

items.title: Police: Pahang recorded 9,833 road accidents in 2025, 108 of them fatal
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 19 — A total of 9,833 road accidents were recorded in Pahang throughout 2025, with 108 cases classified as fatal, claiming 115 lives. Kuantan district police chief ACP Ashari Abu Samah said that from January to February this year, 802 accidents were recorded, including four fatal crashes that resulted in four deaths. Among the areas frequently recording accidents in Kuantan is the East Coast Expressway (LPT), which has the highest number of incidents,” he said.

items.title: LLM: Traffic to East Coast and north congested but under control this morning
items.summary: A spokesman for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said on East Coast route, congestion occurred on the Middle Ring Road 2 towards the Gombak Toll Plaza as well as from Bukit Tinggi, Lentang to the Bentong Petronas Station and Sungai Dua to the Karak Toll Plaza. “For the route to the north, congestion occurred on the North-South Expressway from Slim River to Tapah, Gopeng to Simpang Pulai, Ipoh North to the Menora Tunnel, Bandar Baharu to Jawi, Bukit Tambun to Bukit Minyak and Juru to Perai,” he said when contacted here today. He said for the route to the south, congestion occurred from Port Dickson interchange to Senawang and Pasir Gudang to Setia Tropika.

items.title: Sending international parcels? Pos Malaysia just added a 40pc fuel surcharge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Pos Malaysia has announced a significant new fuel surcharge of up to 40 per cent for international shipments and 15 per cent for key domestic services, citing the dramatic spike in transportation costs. Effective from March 18 until March 29, the national postal service will impose a 40 per cent fuel surcharge on all international shipments, including its popular Express Mail Service (EMS), Air Parcel, and Small Packet services. For domestic routes, a 15 per cent surcharge will be added to the existing domestic fuel surcharge.

items.title: Cops nab two men suspected of stealing TM cables in Teluk Intan
items.summary: IPOH, March 19 — Police have arrested two men suspected of being involved in the theft of Telekom Malaysia (TM) cables during an operation at Jalan Pasir Berdamar, Teluk Intan, three days ago. The Hilir Perak District Police said in a statement on its Facebook page tonight that interrogation of both suspects led to the seizure of a white sack containing coiled copper cables weighing an estimated 5.3 kilogrammes. Police are continuing investigations to identify the suspects’ involvement in other cable theft cases around the Teluk Intan district.

items.title: Kg Pulau Ketam fire destroys five homes, PMO pledges to fund reconstruction
items.summary: KANGAR, March 19 — The Prime Minister’s Office (PMO), through the Federal Development Office, Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU) of the Prime Minister’s Department (JPM) in Perlis, will assist in building five new houses for the victims whose homes were destroyed in a fire in Kampung Pulau Ketam, Kuala Perlis, near here, on Tuesday. Perlis Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Noor Amin Ahmad said that the five houses that were destroyed in the fire will be rebuilt with an allocation of RM80,000 for each house. Also participating in the event was Perlis ICU JPM director Maznah Musa.

items.title: West Asia conflict: Evacuation operations suspended as airspace reopens, Malaysians advised to use commercial flights
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — The evacuation operation for Malaysians from the West Asia conflict zone has been suspended following the reopening of airspace in most countries in the region. “The government always prioritises the safety and welfare of Malaysians abroad. On March 11, a total of 163 Malaysians who were previously stranded in several countries in West Asia safely returned home via a special Malaysia Airlines flight under the Malaysian Evacuation Operation from the West Asia conflict zone.

items.title: Border survey team member missing after being swept away by river currents in Ulu Temburong, says Sarawak Fire Dept
items.summary: LAWAS, March 19 — A man has been reported missing after he was believed to have been swept away by strong river currents while attempting to cross a river in the Ulu Temburong area. The victim, identified as Donysius Gabriel Goh, 36, was part of a Malaysian survey team, involving five other colleagues, conducting operations along the Malaysia-Brunei international border. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department said it received an emergency call at 9.19 pm, prompting a response team from the Lawas Fire and Rescue Station to be deployed to the scene.

items.title: Suspect in Cikgu Chandra attack case alleges torture in police custody
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — A suspect in the high-profile attack on social media personality Cikgu Chandra claims he was beaten, electrocuted, and tortured by police to force a confession while being interrogated at the Serdang district police headquarters. The 40-year-old tow-truck operator lodged a police report on Tuesday, claiming that he was beaten multiple times during interrogation. “My hands were handcuffed behind my back and were stepped on by several officers until I screamed in pain,” the man alleged in a police report.

items.title: Police carry out three traffic dispersal ops in Gua Musang to ease Aidilfitri congestion
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, March 19 — Police carried out three traffic dispersal operations at several key locations, following an increase in vehicles due to those returning to their hometowns for Hari Raya Aidilfitri, entering the state via Jalan Kuala Lipis, Pahang-Gua Musang yesterday. Gua Musang district police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said the locations involved were the Merapoh border in Pahang, namely Kampung Mentara; the Gua Musang-Kuala Krai route near Masjid Buluh; and Jalan Persiaran Raya. However, he said traffic flow was slightly smoother along the Gua Musang-Kuala Krai route near Kem Semur.

items.title: LLM ramps up Aidilfitri preparations with deployment of emergency response teams, activates 873 CCTVs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — The Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) continues to enhance its preparedness by deploying Emergency Response Teams (ERTs) at 12 strategic locations and activating 873 closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras throughout the country’s highway network to monitor traffic movements in conjunction with the Aidilfitri celebrations. LLM director-general Ir Mohd Hadzmir Yusoff said these measures are part of the agency’s maximum preparedness to handle the expected surge in traffic, which is projected to reach up to 3.5 million vehicles per day during the festive period. To meet the needs of electric vehicles (EVs), Mohd Hadzmir said LLM is providing charging stations along the routes.

items.title: Heavy post‑iftar traffic yesterday as Rawang crash adds to northbound congestion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Traffic flow on major highways nationwide worsened post-iftar, following a surge in vehicles heading back to hometowns for the Aidilfitri celebration. A spokesperson for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said significant congestion was detected at the Gombak Toll Plaza, with queues reportedly stretching to the DUKE Highway near Batu Caves. “For the northbound direction, traffic from Rawang Selatan to Bukit Beruntung has been reported to be almost at a standstill,” the spokesperson said when contacted by Bernama last night.

items.title: Fuel prices surge again: RON97 up 70 sen, diesel hits RM4.72 amid US-Iran conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — For the second consecutive week, fuel prices in Peninsular Malaysia have seen a massive spike as the government moves to align domestic rates with a global market rattled by the outbreak of conflict in the Middle East. Effective from today until March 25, RON97 petrol will jump by a staggering 70 sen to RM4.55 per litre, while diesel in the Peninsula will surge by another 80 sen to a record RM4.72 per litre. Additionally, diesel prices across Sabah, Sarawak, and Labuan are being held steady at the regional ceiling of RM2.15 per litre to ensure regional price stability.

items.title: Friday or Saturday? Malaysians ready either way for Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Malaysians are heading into Hari Raya Aidilfitri this year better prepared, with many already bracing for the possibility that the celebration could fall either on Friday or Saturday. That year, Aidilfitri had been calculated to fall on May 3. “This year, my family and I expect it to fall on Saturday or Friday.

items.title: Enjoy your iftar dates now: Local wholesalers say prices, supplies unaffected by Middle East conflict but that could change
items.summary: At Al Badr Trading, a date wholesaler in Danau Kota, Setapak, boxes of imported dates — from Saudi Arabia’s prized Mabroom to Iran’s Mariami — are seen stacked neatly, ready for bulk sale during Ramadan. When kept in cold storage, dates can remain in good condition well past their expiry dates, with a shelf life extending up to one to three years. Laili gesturing at a variety of imported dates from the Middle East being sold at Al Badr Trading in Danau Kota, Setapak.
